entities.name: ANTHRACNOSE
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting sorghum caused by Colletotrichum graminicola

entities.name: COLLETRITRICHUM GRAMINICOLA
entities.description: The fungal pathogen that causes anthracnose in sorghum

entities.name: GREENHOUSE SCREENING
entities.description: A controlled environment technique used to identify anthracnose resistance

entities.name: FIELD SCREENING
entities.description: A technique used in field conditions to identify anthracnose resistance

entities.name: ANTHRACNOSE RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of sorghum lines to resist infection by Colletotrichum graminicola

entities.name: ANTHRACNOSE SEVERITY
entities.description: The measurement of the extent of anthracnose disease development

entities.name: HIGH HUMIDITY
entities.description: Environmental condition of high relative humidity used to facilitate fungal infection

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent where field-screening techniques were transferred

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Country where field-screening techniques were transferred and conducted

entities.name: PANTNAGAR
entities.description: Location in North India where sorghum germplasm was screened

entities.name: BURKINA FASO
entities.description: Country where multilocational tests for anthracnose resistance were conducted

entities.name: NIGERIA
entities.description: Country where multilocational tests for anthracnose resistance were conducted

entities.name: ZAMBIA
entities.description: Country where multilocational tests for anthracnose resistance were conducted

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: Country where multilocational tests for anthracnose resistance were conducted

entities.name: BACKCROSSING
entities.description: A breeding method used to transfer traits into a specific genetic background

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A genetic trait used as a source for creating male-sterile lines

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The inability of a plant to produce functional pollen

relationships.description: used to convert resistant lines into male-sterile lines
